data.title: Wall Street Tops Record Highs As Fed Cuts Rates, Signals More Ahead: This Week In Markets
data.description:
Wall Street climbed to fresh record highs after the Federal Reserve delivered its first interest rate cut in nine months — hinting that more reductions could be on the way.

  • VOO ETF has set new all-time highs this week. Check live chart here.

On Thursday, the S&P 500, Nasdaq 100, Dow Jones and small-cap Russell 2000 all closed at all-time highs as investors celebrated the Fed's move.

Fed Rate Cuts

At its September meeting, the central bank lowered rates by 25 basis points to a target range of 4%–4.25%, in line with expectations, as policymakers shifted their focus toward a cooling labor market.

The decision wasn't unanimous: Fed Gov. Stephen Miran — appointed by President Donald Trump to replace Adriana Kugler, who resigned last month — argued for a deeper 50-basis-point cut.

Fed Chair Jerome Powell called the move a "risk management cut," stressing that the Fed acted preemptively to avoid a sharper labor market slowdown. He also played down inflationary pressures from tariffs, describing them as a temporary, one-off factor in the Fed's baseline outlook.

“A reasonable base case is that the [tariff] effects on inflation will be relatively short-lived,” Powell said.

The Fed's updated projections raised both growth and inflation estimates. Yet its rate forecast painted a more aggressive cutting path: two more reductions by the end of 2025, followed by one each in 2026 and 2027.

How Will Rate Cuts Impact Homebuyers?

Rate-sensitive sectors are already showing signs of revival. Mortgage demand spiked ahead of the Fed's decision, with homebuyers rushing to refinance.

In the week ending Sept. 12, total mortgage applications surged 29.7% from the prior week, while refinancing activity jumped 57.7% — the highest level since March 2022, according to the Mortgage Bankers Association.

Intel’s Surge

Intel Corp. INTC was the standout stock of the week. Shares jumped 23% on Thursday after NVIDIA Corp. NVDIA revealed a $5 billion investment in Intel common stock at $23.28 per share, giving it roughly a 5% stake. The investment is part of a broader partnership to co-develop multiple generations of custom chips for data centers and PCs.

Ford Slumps, Auto Recall

Ford Motor Co. F, meanwhile, posted its third straight weekly loss. The automaker recalled 101,944 U.S. vehicles after regulators warned that faulty door trim could detach while driving, posing a crash risk, according to the National Highway Traffic Safety Administration.

Read Now:

  • Nvidia And Intel Just Changed The AI Game—And The Pressure on AMD And ARM Is Rising

Image created using artificial intelligence via Midjourney.

data.title: Here's How Much $100 Invested In Wells Fargo 5 Years Ago Would Be Worth Today
data.description:
Wells Fargo WFC has outperformed the market over the past 5 years by 14.07% on an annualized basis producing an average annual return of 29.05%. Currently, Wells Fargo has a market capitalization of $272.24 billion.

Buying $100 In WFC: If an investor had bought $100 of WFC stock 5 years ago, it would be worth $352.08 today based on a price of $84.54 for WFC at the time of writing.

Wells Fargo's Performance Over Last 5 Years

Finally -- what's the point of all this? The key insight to take from this article is to note how much of a difference compounded returns can make in your cash growth over a period of time.

data.description:

Predicted Price Range

Below, we can observe the evolution of the volume and open interest of calls and puts, respectively, for all of Energy Transfer's whale activity within a strike price range from $15.0 to $20.0 in the last 30 days.

Energy Transfer Call and Put Volume: 30-Day Overview

Largest Options Trades Observed:

About Energy Transfer

Energy Transfer owns one of the largest portfolios of crude oil, natural gas, and natural gas liquids midstream assets in the US, primarily in Texas and the US midcontinent region. Its system includes more than 12,000 miles of intrastate pipelines and 20,000 miles of interstate pipelines. It also owns gathering, processing, and storage facilities in the largest US oil and gas producing regions. Other businesses include a network of natural gas liquids and refined products facilities, 18,000 miles of crude oil pipelines, and the Lake Charles gas liquefaction facility. Energy Transfer combined its publicly traded limited and general partnerships in October 2018.

After a thorough review of the options trading surrounding Energy Transfer, we move to examine the company in more detail. This includes an assessment of its current market status and performance.

  • Earnings announcement expected in 47 days.

data.title: Options Corner: Why BigBear.ai Bulls Continue To Load Up Despite The Short-Selling Threat
data.description:
While the U.S. economy may be headed toward a period of stagflation, the one sector that continues to not receive the memo is technology; specifically artificial intelligence. Just when you thought that the generative AI boom was losing its charm — particularly from the disappointing response to Nvidia Corp NVDA and its latest earnings report — individual players in the machine learning space have fought back. One name to watch closely is BigBear.ai Holdings Inc BBAI.

On paper, BigBear.ai appeals to investors because of its focus on high-stakes decision environments. Fundamentally, the company forwards solutions tied to decision intelligence, which uses AI, data fusion, sensor networks and other innovations to help organizations make faster, superior decisions — especially under pressure. As such, BigBear.ai's platform offers significant relevance for both commercial and governmental applications.

Of course, I would be remiss to not mention what might really be attracting investors — the BBAI stock price. At time of writing, the security is attempting to march toward the $7 level. That means it's really not that far removed from what many traders would consider penny-stock territory. What's more, purely objective investors would likely declare that BigBear.ai's fundamentals are poor. For example, its quarterly disclosures consistently miss expectations.

So, why does it appear that everyone's gaga over BBAI stock? If the stars align, it could be the next Palantir Technologies Inc PLTR.

It's not just an empty opinion. Goldman Sachs' regulatory filings revealed that the banking juggernaut had increased its holdings of BBAI stock by 569% during the first quarter. It now owns over 2.3 million shares worth about $6.7 million.

Enticingly, there's a case to be made that this value could rise much higher.

BBAI Stock Sets Up A Compelling Showdown

One of the commonly cited statistics of BigBear.ai is that the company has likely attracted a number of pessimistic speculators. Notably, the short interest of BBAI stock stands at 19.8% of its float. To be clear, there is no one metric that defines whether a security suffers from excessively elevated short interest. However, Charles Schwab states that a threshold of 10% or higher may be a warning sign.

Further, the financial institution points out that securities with short interest of 20% or higher would be on the more elevated end of the spectrum.

Now, short interest is an often-misunderstood topic. To really appreciate what's going on, a true short position must be recognized as a credit-based transaction. Shorting a stock means that the speculator is selling the target security to open a bearish position, with the ultimate hope that it falls in value. If it does, the short trader can buy back the deflated shares on a relative discount and return them to the lending broker.

However, because the securities that were sold to initiate the short position were borrowed on credit, the broker must be made whole — irrespective of whatever happens to the stock price. Put another way, a short trade can be very lucrative, so long as the stock falls. If it goes the other way, a panic erupts because the shorts must now exit their position.

By logical deduction, a short position can only be exited by buying to close. That's where the upside pressure from a short squeeze comes into play.

Admittedly, just because BBAI stock has high short interest doesn't necessarily mean that this ratio is 100% allocated to bearish bets. Some of it could simply be hedged transactions.

Still, what's fascinating is that since July 2023, the correlation coefficient between BBAI stock and its price action clocks in at 80.73%. As the short interest has moved up, the security has marched higher — albeit in a choppy manner. It may not be a causal relationship but it's potentially setting up a showdown between the bulls and bears.

A Bull Sees Red

Interestingly, as short interest is rising, BBAI stock appears to be printing a technical pattern known as the bullish pennant. Under the technical discipline, buyers and sellers engage in heated battle until the frontline stagnates, resulting in a triangular geometry. At the focal point, technical analysts believe that there is a higher probability of a breakout move.

To be blunt, it's a heuristic argument, taking into account what other traders might do. But in the case of BBAI stock, it might not be an empty declaration because of the elevated short interest. Since short interest stems from a credit-based transaction, the creditor must be made whole — irrespective of the intent of the trader initiating the short position.

As such, I see an explosive move happening, with a longer-term target of $10. When that might happen, though, is the difficult part to figure out.

Personally, I would consider a staggered strategy, first by securing a position in the open market. This way, you don't have to deal with options and their expiration dates.

As for an options-focused trade, I'm tempted by the $7 call expiring March 20, 2026. At time of writing, the ask is $1.80, while the bid-ask spread as represented by the midpoint is 2.81%. Essentially, this means you're paying 2.8 cents per dollar of option premium to cross the spread, which is quite tight for a speculative entity like BBAI stock.

For those who are dead-set on a vertical spread, the 6.50/7.50 bull call spread expiring Oct. 31 may be intriguing. A capped-risk, capped-reward trade, this geometry will require BBAI stock to rise through the $7.50 price level at expiration. If it does, the payout stands at a tempting 150%.

The opinions and views expressed in this content are those of the individual author and do not necessarily reflect the views of Benzinga. Benzinga is not responsible for the accuracy or reliability of any information provided herein. This content is for informational purposes only and should not be misconstrued as investment advice or a recommendation to buy or sell any security. Readers are asked not to rely on the opinions or information herein, and encouraged to do their own due diligence before making investing decisions.
